scenarios.description: Memory leak in distributed training after upgrading NCCL

context.title: OOM crash during DDP training with NCCL 2.18
context.body: After upgrading NCCL from 2.17 to 2.18, our distributed training jobs crash with OOM after ~200 iterations. The leak seems to come from all-reduce buffers not being freed. Reproducible on 8xA100.

scenarios.description: Feature request for torch.compile support on custom ops

context.title: torch.compile fails silently on custom C++ ops registered via torch.library
context.body: When using torch.compile on a model that uses custom ops registered with torch.library, the compiler silently falls back to eager mode. Expected: either compile the custom op or raise a clear error. This affects PT2 adoption significantly.

scenarios.description: Critical data corruption in checkpoint saving

context.title: torch.save() produces corrupted checkpoints on NFS with >10GB models
context.body: When saving models larger than 10GB to NFS-mounted storage, the checkpoint file is silently corrupted. Loading the checkpoint produces incorrect tensor shapes. This is a data-loss scenario. Affects production training pipelines.

context.title: Docs for torch.autograd.Function lack examples for vmap support
context.body: The documentation for custom autograd functions doesn't cover how to make them compatible with vmap/functorch. Several users have asked about this. Adding 2-3 examples would significantly improve the developer experience.

scenarios.description: Performance regression in conv2d after recent PR

context.title: 20% slowdown in conv2d on CPU after PR #98765
context.body: After merging PR #98765 (memory layout optimization), conv2d performance regressed by ~20% on CPU for batch_size=1. GPU perf is unaffected. Bisected to commit abc123. This blocks the 2.3 release.

scenarios.description: SQL injection in model registry query endpoint
XSS in model card rendering via markdown
SSRF in webhook URL for model deployment notifications

context.code:
import requests
def send_notification(webhook_url: str, message: str):
    response = requests.post(webhook_url, json={'text': message})
    return response.status_code

scenarios.description: Upgrade numpy from 1.24.x to 2.0 (major breaking change)

context.changelog_summary: NumPy 2.0 removes deprecated APIs, changes default integer type to int64, removes numpy.distutils, and changes array string representations.
Pydantic V2 is a full rewrite using pydantic-core. Major changes: BaseModel.dict() → model_dump(), validator → field_validator, Config class → model_config dict.
Flask 3.0 drops Python 3.7 support, removes deprecated code, requires Werkzeug >= 3.0. Blueprints no longer support the `name` argument — use `import_name`.
Requests 2.32.x includes minor bug fixes for session handling and certificate verification. No breaking changes.

scenarios.description: Upgrade transformers 4.36 → 4.40 with new pipeline breaking changes

context.changelog_summary: Transformers 4.40 changes the default attention implementation, deprecates old pipeline names, requires tokenizers >= 0.19. Some model configs renamed.

scenarios.description: Critical Performance: Fix CUDA OOM/Memory leak via PyTorch upgrade

context.changelog_summary: PyTorch 2.3 resolves a major CUDA memory leak in CUDAGraphs and NCCL backends. It significantly reduces memory fragmentation in torch.compile workflows. Essential for large model training.
